scope.pairingTarget: 1-5 per compound when evidence supports it; 0 allowed
scope.medicalAdviceDisclaimer: Catalog research metadata only; not medical advice.

pairings.rationale: BPC-157 is supported by preclinical tendon/angiogenesis literature while thymosin beta-4/TB-500 is associated with actin-mediated cell migration, angiogenesis, and wound healing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Complementary repair pathways: BPC-157 emphasizes angiogenic and fibroblast/tendon signaling; TB-500 emphasizes repair-cell migration and inflammatory resolution.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct high-quality human combination trial found. Avoid presenting as therapeutic human protocol.
pairings.avoidIf: Active malignancy concern, uncontrolled inflammatory disease, pregnancy, or clinician-advised avoidance of experimental peptides.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Keep as a paired research note; do not encode exact co-dosing until implementation review defines protocol safety language.

pairings.rationale: BPC-157 has gut/tissue repair rationale, while KPV has anti-inflammatory activity in murine colitis models and alpha-MSH-derived inflammatory pathways.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Mucosal repair plus local inflammatory tone modulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found; KPV evidence remains primarily preclinical.
pairings.avoidIf: Inflammatory bowel disease flare requiring medical care, immunosuppression, pregnancy, or unknown peptide sensitivity.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Represent as benefit-goal pairing only; do not imply a route or schedule.

pairings.rationale: BPC-157 has tissue-healing and angiogenic preclinical support; GHK-Cu has connective-tissue/collagen accumulation and skin regeneration support.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Repair signaling plus collagen/glycosaminoglycan matrix remodeling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found; avoid claiming accelerated healing in humans.
pairings.avoidIf: Copper metabolism disorders, active malignancy concern, or significant local skin reactions.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Could render as skin/connective-tissue pairing with conservative language.

pairings.rationale: Same reciprocal repair pairing as BPC-157 plus TB-500.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Cell migration/angiogenesis plus tendon/fibroblast repair signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct high-quality human combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Active malignancy concern, pregnancy, or clinician-advised avoidance of experimental peptides.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Use reciprocal pairing display; do not duplicate citations in UI if deduping pairings later.

pairings.rationale: TB-500/thymosin beta-4 literature supports dermal healing and repair-cell migration; GHK-Cu supports connective tissue accumulation and skin remodeling.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Migration and wound-closure support plus extracellular matrix rebuilding.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Mechanistic only; no direct combination outcome trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Copper metabolism disorders, active malignancy concern, or significant skin irritation.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Treat as dermal/wound research pairing, not a protocol.

pairings.rationale: Cagrilintide plus semaglutide has direct randomized human evidence as CagriSema, combining amylin and GLP-1 mechanisms.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Distinct satiety and gastric-emptying pathways can produce greater weight loss than either component alone in trials.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Additive GI adverse events, dehydration risk, hypoglycemia risk when used with insulin/secretagogues, pancreatitis/gallbladder warning context.
pairings.avoidIf: Personal/family MTC or MEN2 risk, severe gastroparesis, pancreatitis history unless clinician-reviewed, pregnancy, or concurrent GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agonist use.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Represent as coadministration evidence, but do not encode self-mixing or dosing instructions.

pairings.rationale: GHK-Cu targets skin repair and collagen/matrix biology, while Snap-8/acetyl-octapeptide logic overlaps the cosmetic expression-line peptide category.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Matrix remodeling plus expression-line modulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Most evidence is topical/cosmetic, not systemic. Skin penetration and formulation matter.
pairings.avoidIf: Copper metabolism disorders, active dermatitis at application site, or sensitivity to cosmetic peptides.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Prefer topical/cosmetic framing; avoid implying injectable cosmetic protocol.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal skin/connective-tissue repair pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Collagen/matrix support plus angiogenic/tendon repair signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Copper metabolism disorders, active malignancy concern, pregnancy, or significant local reactions.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Pairing should be displayed as research rationale only.

pairings.rationale: Tesamorelin is a GHRH analog and ipamorelin is a selective GHS-R agonist; GHRH + GHRP/GHS pathway complementarity supports GH pulse amplification.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Pituitary GHRH receptor stimulation plus ghrelin/GHS receptor stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Monitor IGF-1/glucose; avoid redundant GH-axis exposure, edema, carpal tunnel symptoms, and sleep apnea worsening.
pairings.avoidIf: Active malignancy, uncontrolled diabetes, untreated sleep apnea, elevated IGF-1, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Cautious GH-axis pairing; not best overall because tesamorelin is long-acting and indication-specific.

pairings.rationale: Limited human cohort data studied thymic and pineal peptide bioregulators together for geroprotective outcomes.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Pineal/circadian regulatory support plus thymic/immune-aging support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Older non-Western cohort evidence; not a modern randomized standard. Avoid mortality-benefit claims in UI.
pairings.avoidIf: Active malignancy concern, autoimmune instability, pregnancy, or use outside clinician-supervised research context.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as limited human evidence with no therapeutic claim.

pairings.rationale: Epitalon is associated with pineal/circadian biology, while DSIP has small human sleep studies with mixed results.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Circadian support plus sleep-architecture support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: DSIP evidence is mixed, including a small negative insomnia study. Avoid strong claims.
pairings.avoidIf: Severe sleep apnea, sedative polypharmacy, pregnancy, or unexplained hypersomnia.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Sequence/timing should be deferred to clinical review; UI should show mixed evidence.

pairings.rationale: MOTS-c literature centers on mitochondrial stress/metabolic signaling, while NAD+ is a core redox and sirtuin/PARP substrate in aging biology.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Mitochondrial signaling plus cellular redox/coenzyme availability.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination clinical trial found; avoid claiming additive energy or fat-loss outcomes.
pairings.avoidIf: Active cancer treatment unless oncology-reviewed, pregnancy, severe flushing/nausea reactions, or unstable metabolic disease.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Keep as conceptual mitochondrial pairing; adjunct methylation supports are deferred.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal gut inflammation/repair pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Mucosal repair plus local inflammatory-tone modulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Acute severe GI symptoms requiring medical care, pregnancy, or unknown peptide sensitivity.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Represent as gut-focused research pairing only.

pairings.rationale: KPV has anti-inflammatory preclinical support; LL-37 is a host-defense peptide with antimicrobial and immunomodulatory actions.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Inflammation modulation plus host-defense signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: LL-37 can participate in inflammatory disease pathways; avoid simple 'immune boost' framing.
pairings.avoidIf: Autoimmune flares, psoriasis/rosacea-like inflammatory skin disease, pregnancy, or active infection needing medical care.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Use only as conservative immune/mucosal pairing.

pairings.rationale: Thymosin alpha-1 has human clinical immunomodulatory literature; LL-37 is an endogenous antimicrobial/immunomodulatory peptide.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Adaptive immune modulation plus innate host-defense peptide signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found. Avoid use in active autoimmune instability without specialist review.
pairings.avoidIf: Autoimmune flare, transplant immunosuppression, active severe infection without care, pregnancy.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as immune-support research pairing, not infection treatment.

pairings.rationale: Thymosin alpha-1 modulates immune response, while KPV has anti-inflammatory melanocortin-derived activity.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Immune calibration plus inflammatory signaling modulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Autoimmune instability, pregnancy, or active infection requiring medical treatment.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Avoid broad claims; keep as inflammation-balance pairing.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal sleep/circadian pairing with mixed evidence.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Sleep-pressure/sleep-architecture support plus circadian/pineal support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: DSIP evidence is mixed and small. Avoid strong claims.
pairings.avoidIf: Severe sleep apnea, sedative polypharmacy, pregnancy, or unexplained hypersomnia.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Display mixed evidence caveat.

pairings.rationale: Hexarelin is a GHS-R agonist and Sermorelin is a GHRH analog; GHRH + GHRP literature supports synergistic GH secretion.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GHS-R stimulation plus GHRH receptor stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Hexarelin has greater prolactin/cortisol and desensitization concerns than ipamorelin.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, untreated sleep apnea, active malignancy, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Cycle/rest language should be reviewed before implementation.

pairings.rationale: CJC-1295 is a GHRH analog candidate that would complement Hexarelin's GHS-R activity.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Longer GHRH receptor drive plus GHS-R stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Missing compound; must distinguish DAC vs no-DAC. High GH/IGF-1 exposure risk.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not add until CJC-1295 is modeled and reviewed.

pairings.rationale: hCG can maintain intratesticular testosterone during testosterone-induced gonadotropin suppression and is used in fertility-preservation contexts.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Exogenous testosterone symptom replacement plus LH-like testicular signaling support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Can raise estradiol, acne, gynecomastia, mood changes, and polycythemia risk context. Requires clinician monitoring.
pairings.avoidIf: Active prostate/breast cancer, untreated severe sleep apnea, high hematocrit, uncontrolled heart failure, or no fertility/testicular-preservation goal.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: UI should avoid protocol dosing and suggest semen/lab monitoring in future monitoring-support phase.

pairings.rationale: Combination testosterone and PDE5 inhibitor therapy has clinical literature in hypogonadal men with erectile dysfunction.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Androgen deficiency correction plus PDE5-mediated erectile hemodynamic support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: PDE5 hypotension risk; testosterone requires hematocrit, prostate, sleep apnea, and cardiovascular context review.
pairings.avoidIf: Nitrate use, riociguat use, unstable cardiovascular disease, severe hypotension, high hematocrit, active prostate/breast cancer.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not pair tadalafil with vardenafil; one PDE5 inhibitor at a time.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal PDE5 inhibitor plus testosterone pairing for hypogonadal ED.
pairings.expectedSynergy: PDE5-mediated erectile response plus androgen repletion when testosterone deficiency is present.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Nitrates are contraindicated. Do not combine multiple PDE5 inhibitors.
pairings.avoidIf: Nitrate use, riociguat use, unstable cardiovascular disease, severe hypotension, or concurrent vardenafil.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Future UI should show red 'do not combine with nitrates' warning.

pairings.rationale: Class-level evidence supports testosterone plus PDE5 inhibitors for selected hypogonadal ED patients; vardenafil-specific pairing evidence is weaker than tadalafil.
pairings.expectedSynergy: PDE5-mediated erectile response plus androgen repletion when testosterone deficiency is present.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Vardenafil has nitrate contraindication and QT/CYP3A4 caveats in labeling.
pairings.avoidIf: Nitrate use, riociguat use, unstable cardiovascular disease, congenital long QT, class IA/III antiarrhythmics, strong CYP3A4 inhibitors, or concurrent tadalafil.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: One PDE5 inhibitor at a time.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal thymic/pineal bioregulator pairing from limited human cohort literature.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Thymic immune-aging support plus pineal/circadian regulatory support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Older cohort data; avoid mortality-benefit claims.
pairings.avoidIf: Active malignancy concern, autoimmune instability, pregnancy, or use outside clinician-supervised research context.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Display as limited evidence.

pairings.rationale: Selank/Semax pairing logic combines anxiolytic stress modulation with Semax neurotrophic/cognitive mechanisms; amidated Selank evidence is extrapolated.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Calm focus: anxiety-buffering plus cognitive/neurotrophic signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Evidence for the amidated analog pairing is weaker than parent Selank/Semax literature.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or concurrent psychoactive changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Prefer conservative cognitive/anxiolytic wording.

pairings.rationale: Analog-to-analog version of the Selank/Semax cognitive/anxiolytic pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Stress-buffering plus cognitive/neurotrophic signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Extrapolated from parent compounds; no direct robust human analog-combination data found.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or psychoactive medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Mark as low-confidence analog extrapolation.

pairings.rationale: hCG supplies LH-like signaling and hMG supplies FSH/LH activity; combined gonadotropin therapy is established for spermatogenesis induction in male hypogonadotropic hypogonadism.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Leydig-cell testosterone support plus Sertoli-cell/spermatogenesis support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Requires semen, testosterone, estradiol, LH/FSH, and testicular-volume context; can raise estradiol and gynecomastia risk.
pairings.avoidIf: Primary testicular failure without specialist plan, active hormone-sensitive cancer, pregnancy exposure concerns, or unsupervised fertility treatment.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Future monitoring-support model should attach semen analysis and hormone labs.

pairings.rationale: hCG can support intratesticular testosterone during testosterone-induced gonadotropin suppression.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Symptom replacement plus testicular signaling support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Requires clinician monitoring and fertility-goal context.
pairings.avoidIf: Active hormone-sensitive cancer, high hematocrit, uncontrolled sleep apnea, or no fertility/testicular-preservation goal.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Avoid dosing language; future monitoring-support model should include semen analysis.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal combined gonadotropin pairing for spermatogenesis support.
pairings.expectedSynergy: FSH/LH activity plus LH-like hCG support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Requires fertility-specialist context and labs.
pairings.avoidIf: Primary testicular failure without specialist plan or active hormone-sensitive cancer.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Attach monitoring requirements in later phase.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal innate/adaptive immune-modulation pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Host-defense peptide signaling plus thymic immune modulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Autoimmune flare, transplant immunosuppression, severe active infection without medical care, pregnancy.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not present as infection treatment.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal mucosal immune-defense plus inflammatory-tone pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Host defense plus anti-inflammatory signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: LL-37 has complex pro- and anti-inflammatory roles.
pairings.avoidIf: Autoimmune flare, inflammatory skin disease flare, pregnancy, or active infection needing medical care.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Use careful caveat language.

pairings.rationale: Selank has anxiolytic/non-sedating research support; Semax has neurotrophic/BDNF preclinical support and limited non-US clinical use.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Cognitive drive plus anxiety-buffering.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No robust Western direct combination trial found; psychoactive response can vary.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as calm-focus research pairing.

pairings.rationale: Parent Selank plus amidated Semax analog pairing extrapolates the Selank/Semax calm-focus logic.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Anxiolytic buffer plus cognitive/neurotrophic signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Analog-specific direct evidence is weak.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Mark as analog extrapolation.

pairings.rationale: Sermorelin is a GHRH analog and Ipamorelin is a selective GHS-R agonist; GHRH + GHRP literature supports synergistic GH secretion.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Physiologic GH pulse prompting through two receptor families.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Monitor IGF-1/glucose; avoid redundant GH-axis exposure.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Best current in-catalog GH secretagogue pairing; still needs conservative UI language.

pairings.rationale: GHRP-2 is a GHS-R agonist that can complement Sermorelin's GHRH receptor activity.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Higher GH pulse signaling than either pathway alone.
pairings.safetyCaveats: More appetite/cortisol/prolactin concerns than ipamorelin.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Secondary to Ipamorelin because selectivity is less favorable.

pairings.rationale: GHRP-6 can complement Sermorelin's GHRH receptor activity and adds appetite-support rationale.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GH pulse amplification plus appetite support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Hunger/cravings, water retention, and desensitization concerns.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, binge-eating risk, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Use only when appetite support is a desired benefit goal.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal cosmetic skin pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Expression-line modulation plus matrix/collagen support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Cosmetic/topical evidence; penetration and formulation matter.
pairings.avoidIf: Copper metabolism disorders, active dermatitis, or sensitivity to cosmetic peptides.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Prefer topical/cosmetic framing.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal calm-focus pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Neurotrophic/cognitive signaling plus anxiolytic buffering.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No robust Western direct combination trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Render as cognitive/anxiolytic balance.

pairings.rationale: Parent Semax plus amidated Selank analog pairing extrapolates the Semax/Selank calm-focus logic.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Cognitive/neurotrophic signaling plus anxiolytic buffering.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Analog-specific direct evidence is weak.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Mark as analog extrapolation.

pairings.rationale: Amidated Semax analog plus parent Selank pairing extrapolates the Semax/Selank calm-focus logic.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Cognitive/neurotrophic signaling plus anxiolytic buffering.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Analog-specific direct evidence is weak.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Mark as analog extrapolation.

pairings.rationale: Analog-to-analog version of Semax/Selank pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Cognitive/neurotrophic signaling plus anxiolytic buffering.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Direct evidence is weak and extrapolated.
pairings.avoidIf: Bipolar/mania risk, severe anxiety destabilization, pregnancy, or medication changes without clinician review.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Mark as low-confidence analog extrapolation.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal mitochondrial/metabolic pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Redox/coenzyme substrate availability plus mitochondrial stress/metabolic signaling.
pairings.safetyCaveats: No direct combination clinical trial found.
pairings.avoidIf: Active cancer treatment unless oncology-reviewed, pregnancy, severe flushing/nausea reactions, or unstable metabolic disease.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Adjunct methylation supports are deferred.

pairings.rationale: GHRP-2 GHS-R activity complements Sermorelin GHRH receptor activity.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GH pulse amplification through two receptor pathways.
pairings.safetyCaveats: GHRP-2 may raise hunger, cortisol, or prolactin more than ipamorelin.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Secondary to Ipamorelin-based pairing because selectivity is less favorable.

pairings.rationale: CJC-1295 is a missing GHRH analog candidate for GHRP-2 pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GHRH receptor drive plus GHS-R stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Missing compound; DAC/no-DAC distinction required. High GH/IGF-1 exposure risk.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not add until CJC-1295 profile is modeled and reviewed.

pairings.rationale: GHRP-6 GHS-R activity complements Sermorelin GHRH receptor activity.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GH pulse amplification plus appetite support.
pairings.safetyCaveats: GHRP-6 has stronger hunger/craving and water-retention caveats.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, binge-eating risk, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Use when appetite support is a desired benefit goal.

pairings.rationale: CJC-1295 is a missing GHRH analog candidate for GHRP-6 pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GHRH receptor drive plus GHS-R stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Missing compound; DAC/no-DAC distinction required. High GH/IGF-1 exposure and appetite/glucose risk.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, binge-eating risk, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not add until CJC-1295 profile is modeled and reviewed.

pairings.rationale: Ipamorelin is a selective GHS-R agonist and Sermorelin is a GHRH analog; this is the cleanest current in-catalog GH secretagogue pairing.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Selective GHS-R stimulation plus GHRH receptor stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Monitor IGF-1/glucose; avoid redundant GH-axis exposure.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Best current in-catalog GH pairing but still not direct outcome evidence.

pairings.rationale: CJC-1295 is a missing GHRH analog candidate for the common Ipamorelin pairing pattern.
pairings.expectedSynergy: GHRH receptor drive plus selective GHS-R stimulation.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Missing compound; DAC/no-DAC distinction required. High GH/IGF-1 exposure risk.
pairings.avoidIf: Elevated IGF-1, uncontrolled diabetes, active malignancy, untreated sleep apnea, pregnancy, or concurrent HGH.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not add until CJC-1295 profile is modeled and reviewed.

pairings.rationale: Reciprocal CagriSema pairing with direct randomized human evidence.
pairings.expectedSynergy: Amylin-receptor agonism plus GLP-1 receptor agonism.
pairings.safetyCaveats: Additive GI adverse events, dehydration, gallbladder/pancreatitis warning context, and glucose interactions.
pairings.avoidIf: Personal/family MTC or MEN2 risk, severe gastroparesis, pancreatitis history unless clinician-reviewed, pregnancy, or concurrent GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agonist use.
pairings.timingOrSequencingNotes: Do not encode self-mixing or dosing instructions.

noPairingRecommendations.reason: No responsible compound-to-compound pairing found. Avoid overlapping incretin therapy with other GLP-1/GIP/glucagon agents.
No pairing proposed without indication-specific neuropathy/inflammation review.
Already a fixed combination. Additional incretin/amylin pairings would be redundant and risk-additive.
Triple receptor activity already overlaps with GLP-1/GIP/glucagon pathways; no safe evidence-backed add-on pairing found.
Already a cosmetic blend; best adjuncts are non-compound supports deferred to the adjunct phase.
Current evidence is mainly preclinical senolytic research; common senolytic adjuncts need a separate safety model.
Renal toxicity and experimental status dominate; no benefit-maximizing compound pairing recommended.
Safety profile and anabolic-steroid risk dominate; no benefit-maximizing pairing recommended.
Safety profile, hepatotoxicity, endocrine suppression, and estrogenic risk dominate; no benefit-maximizing pairing recommended.
Direct GH/IGF-1 exposure makes GH secretagogue pairings redundant and risk-additive.
Potential social/anxiolytic pairings are too speculative without psychiatric/endocrine indication review.
Already a cosmetic blend containing GHK-Cu and SNAP-8-like support; further cosmetic peptide pairing is likely redundant.

missingCompoundsProposed.name: CJC-1295 / Modified GRF(1-29)
missingCompoundsProposed.rationale: Existing catalog GH secretagogues repeatedly point to the missing GHRH analog pairing family. Human mechanistic evidence exists for CJC-1295 raising GH and IGF-1, but DAC/no-DAC representation must be decided before adding.

deferredCandidates.name: TMG
deferredCandidates.reason: Relevant to NAD+ methylation support but out of scope for compound-only phase.

deferredCandidates.name: Hyaluronic acid
deferredCandidates.reason: Relevant to GHK-Cu/GLOW topical skin support but out of scope for compound-only phase.

deferredCandidates.name: Quercetin
deferredCandidates.reason: Common senolytic adjunct with FOXO4-DRI context but requires adjunct safety model.

deferredCandidates.name: Dasatinib
deferredCandidates.reason: Senolytic medication candidate; requires medication-specific safety model and is out of scope.

deferredCandidates.name: Melatonin and light-timing protocols
deferredCandidates.reason: Relevant to Epitalon/DSIP but belongs in the later adjunct phase.

deferredCandidates.name: Hydration/electrolyte and constipation management
deferredCandidates.reason: Important for GLP-1/amylin compounds but not a compound pairing.

deferredCandidates.name: Lab monitoring panels
deferredCandidates.reason: IGF-1, glucose, A1c, semen analysis, renal markers, liver enzymes, hematocrit, and hormones should be modeled separately.

safetyExclusions.rule: Do not combine incretin agents with each other unless direct combination evidence exists.
safetyExclusions.rationale: Overlapping GLP-1/GIP/glucagon activity increases GI, dehydration, gallbladder, pancreatitis, and glucose risk.

safetyExclusions.rule: Do not combine PDE5 inhibitors with nitrates or with each other.
safetyExclusions.rationale: Hypotension risk is potentially severe; concurrent PDE5 inhibitor use is redundant.

safetyExclusions.rule: Do not pair HGH with GH secretagogues in the catalog.
safetyExclusions.rationale: Redundant GH/IGF-1 exposure with glucose, edema, sleep apnea, and malignancy-risk uncertainty.

safetyExclusions.rule: Do not create benefit-maximizing anabolic steroid stacks.
safetyExclusions.rationale: Hepatic, lipid, endocrine, fertility, androgenic, and cardiovascular risks require medical framing, not benefit-maximizing catalog pairings.
